response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong testing culture and broad polyglot experience, but falls well short of the role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ programming; the resume shows only ~7 months of professional C++ work (National Instruments) plus a thesis project. There is no evidence of leading technical strategy for a compute platform, no formal technical-leadership or mentoring-of-cloud-engineers role, and cloud/infrastructure exposure is limited to application-level work (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment of a side project) rather than building large-scale infrastructure.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus open-source testing tools authored
Teaching and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, conference talks) relevant to mentoring
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ experience — only a brief stint plus hobby/thesis use
No demonstrated experience building or leading a cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud platform / infrastructure leadership
